messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following public sector local government privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"The Council collects your name, address, council tax account details, and household composition to administer council tax and local services. Processing is authorised under the Local Government Finance Act 1992 and is necessary for the performance of a public task. Data is shared with the Valuation Office Agency and the Department for Work and Pensions as required by law. Records are retained in line with our published retention schedule available at council.gov.uk/retention. You have the right to access, correct, restrict, and object to your data. Our DPO is at dpo@council.gov.uk. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Public task (Art. 6(1)(e)) stated with specific legislative authority (Local Government Finance Act 1992). This is best practice for public sector processing.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Household composition may reveal family structure and disability status. No acknowledgement or specific safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Council tax administration and local services delivery are clear and specific purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for council tax administration.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Retention schedule referenced and publicly available.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), restriction (Art. 18), and objection (Art. 21) stated. Erasure (Art. 17) and portability (Art. 20) absent — note these rights are legitimately limited for public task processing, but this should be explained.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] VOA and DWP named as statutory recipients with legal basis.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Not applicable. No international transfers indicated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@council.gov.uk. Public authorities are required to appoint a DPO under Art. 37(1)(a).

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (household composition may include sensitive data — not addressed), Art. 15-21 (erasure and portability rights absent without explanation)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that household composition may include disability or health-related information and confirm appropriate safeguards
- Add a note explaining that erasure and portability rights are limited for public task processing and direct individuals to the right to object instead
